summary: Details the process of defining and managing the various stages an admission lead will traverse. It explains that 'Approved' and 'Rejected' are default, backend-managed stages, while other custom stages like "Application Submission", "Document Upload", and "Counselor Doc Verification" can be manually added.
navigation: Admission module > Admission Setup > Admission Master > Stage Master Setup
steps: Observe the default stages ("Approved" and "Rejected"), which are system-generated and cannot be modified or removed.
To add a new custom stage, click "Add New".

Enter the desired name for the stage in the "Enter Value" field (e.g., "Application Submission", "Document Upload", "Counselor Doc Verification", "Academic Doc Verification", "Transport and Hostel Selection").
Repeat for all necessary custom stages.
business_rules: "Approved" and "Rejected" are final, default stages provided by the system backend and cannot be created or removed manually.
important_notes: This setup defines the workflow for admission leads.
Stages can be specific to different verification steps or actions.
questions: How do I define custom stages for the admission process workflow?
What are the default stages in the Admission module's Stage Master?
Can I remove the "Approved" or "Rejected" stages?
related_topics: Stage Field Configuration, Counselor Assignment, and Communication Automation
